text.title: integration-testing Core Rules
text.summary: Nine testable rules for real-dependency integration testing with Testcontainers and transaction rollback.

rule.statement: Use real databases in integration tests — never mock the ORM, session, or connection. Testcontainers spins up real Postgres/MySQL/Redis in Docker for the test session.
rule.rationale: Mocking the database defeats the purpose of integration testing: ORM queries, transactions, and schema migrations don't get exercised.

rule.statement: Each test must roll back its changes via a transaction-scoped fixture. Never rely on teardown deletion — it leaks rows when tests fail mid-run.
rule.rationale: BEGIN ... ROLLBACK is atomic and survives test crashes; manual cleanup orphans rows on assertion failure.

rule.statement: Pin container image tags (postgres:16.2, never postgres:latest). Unpinned tags break the suite when a new major version drops in CI.
rule.rationale: :latest is a moving target; pinning ensures reproducible test runs.

rule.statement: Keep unit tests and integration tests in separate directories (tests/unit/ and tests/integration/). Run unit on every save; integration on push/PR only.
rule.rationale: Mixing them slows the inner dev loop and tempts skipping the slow tests.

rule.statement: Integration test suites exceeding 5 minutes must be split or parallelized. Track CI runtime in PR descriptions.
rule.rationale: Suites > 5min get skipped; the rule forces parallelization or scope reduction.

rule.statement: Target 70% unit tests, 20% integration tests, 10% E2E tests. Test failure paths (timeouts, 5xx, retries), not just happy paths.
rule.rationale: Inverted pyramid (more integration than unit) makes the suite slow and brittle.

rule.statement: Use a session-scoped engine fixture for Testcontainers + a function-scoped transaction-rollback session per test. Never create a new container per test.
rule.rationale: Container boot is ~3-5s; per-test containers slow the suite 100x.

rule.statement: Override the get_db dependency to inject the test db_session. Clear dependency_overrides after the test via yield + app.dependency_overrides.clear().
rule.rationale: Without clearing, overrides leak across tests; clearing keeps isolation.

rule.statement: Assert HTTP status AND body shape. Never assert only the status code — a 200 with an empty body is a bug.
rule.rationale: Status-only assertions hide regressions where the route returns success but the wrong payload.

rule.statement: Pinned image, session container, tx rollback, separate dirs, DI override, status+body asserts.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion target for the positive branch.

rule.statement: No real dependencies, no Docker access, or no DI override mechanism.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion target for the negative branch.
